name: International Fire Code 2021
copyright_notice: International Fire Code® 2021 © 2021 International Code Council, Inc.

rules.label: Fire Apparatus Access Roads
rules.notes_template: Verify fire apparatus access roads: ≥ 20 ft unobstructed width, 13 ft 6 in. vertical clearance, 75,000 lb live load capacity, dead-end > 150 ft requires turnaround, within 150 ft of all portions of facilities.
rules.citation: IFC 2021 §503 – Fire apparatus access road minimum 20 ft unobstructed width, 13 ft 6 in. vertical clearance, within 150 ft of all portions of the facility.
rules.remediation: Plan fire access roads per §503; coordinate turnarounds and grade.

rules.label: Fire Hydrant Spacing & Flow
rules.notes_template: Verify fire hydrants per Appendix C: number/spacing/flow set by required fire-flow (Appendix B) and building area; typical commercial spacing ≤ 400–500 ft.
rules.citation: IFC 2021 §507 / Appendices B & C – Fire-flow and hydrant location requirements based on building area, type of construction, and exposures.
rules.remediation: Coordinate civil drawings with hydrant spacing and required flow per AHJ-adopted IFC appendices.

rules.label: Emergency Responder Communication Coverage
rules.notes_template: Verify in-building two-way radio coverage in new buildings (per §510 and AHJ amendments): typically 90% general areas / 95% critical areas at AHJ frequencies, with BDA/DAS as needed.
rules.citation: IFC 2021 §510 – New buildings shall have approved radio coverage for emergency responders.
rules.remediation: Conduct an in-building radio coverage survey; design a BDA/DAS where needed.

rules.label: Maximum Occupant Load Posting
rules.notes_template: Verify maximum occupant load is posted in conspicuous location near each main exit of assembly spaces.
rules.citation: IFC 2021 §1004.9 – Every assembly space shall have its occupant load posted in a conspicuous place.
rules.remediation: Provide posted occupant-load placards in each assembly space.

rules.label: Hazardous Materials Storage / Use
rules.notes_template: If hazardous materials are stored or used, verify quantities against §50 thresholds; exceeding maximum allowable quantities (MAQ) requires Group H classification and §§57–67 chapter compliance.
rules.citation: IFC 2021 Chapters 50–67 – Hazardous-material storage, use, and dispensing thresholds and protection requirements.
rules.remediation: Build a hazmat inventory; classify against MAQ tables; provide control areas and protection per applicable chapter.
